pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 19 creates PROJECT_DIR at this exact path
setup_task.sh line 97 creates target file at PROJECT_DIR + this relative path
Confirmed as deprecated API since Android API 29 (Android 10) per official Android documentation. Direct filesystem access deprecated due to Scoped Storage requirements.
Confirmed as legacy path incompatible with Scoped Storage implementation. Official Android docs recommend MediaStore API instead of direct filesystem access.
Verified via official Android documentation (developer.android.com/reference/android/provider/MediaStore.Images.Media). Required for Scoped Storage media file handling.
Verified via Android documentation and Scoped Storage examples. ContentValues stores media metadata (MIME_TYPE, DISPLAY_NAME, RELATIVE_PATH).
Verified via official Android documentation at developer.android.com/reference/android/provider/MediaStore.Images.Media. This is the content:// URI for primary external storage volume images.
Verified via ContentResolver documentation and Scoped Storage guides. contentResolver.insert() and contentResolver.openOutputStream() are the correct methods for MediaStore operations.
Verified via official ContentResolver documentation and MediaStore examples. contentResolver.openOutputStream(uri) returns OutputStream for writing bitmap data to MediaStore URI.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 122 and verifier.py line 74. This is the RELATIVE_PATH value for MediaStore.MediaColumns.RELATIVE_PATH in ContentValues.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires refactoring ImageExporter.kt to replace deprecated Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory() with MediaStore API for Android 10+ Scoped Storage compliance. The project directory is /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/PhotoStamp with target file at app/src/main/java/com/example/photostamp/ImageExporter.kt. The correct implementation must use ContentValues to set MIME type 'image/jpeg' and RELATIVE_PATH 'Pictures/PhotoStamp', insert into MediaStore.Images.Media.EXTERNAL_CONTENT_URI via contentResolver.insert(), and write bitmap data via contentResolver.openOutputStream(). The forbidden legacy terms are getExternalStorageDirectory and /sdcard/. All required terms (MediaStore, ContentValues, EXTERNAL_CONTENT_URI, contentResolver, openOutputStream) are standard Android Scoped Storage APIs verified in official documentation.
